name: La Tupina
summary: A classic Bordeaux/Southwest France destination meal for after the Bordeaux stage, with official reservations and hours.
whyItMatters: This is the Bordeaux meal with the clearest sense of place: fire, Southwest products, old-city atmosphere, and a long local reputation.
pros: Official site lists reservation link, address, phone, and hours
Strong regional identity around cuisine from the South-West
Open Friday lunch and dinner under normal hours, matching Stage 7 day
cons: Likely reservation-sensitive during a Tour de France finish in Bordeaux
Hearty meal may be better for dinner than a hot race-day lunch

logistics.reservation_needed: Yes, recommended; official site links to reservations.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging is confirmed; near Porte de la Monnaie/Saint-Michel side of central Bordeaux.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Unknown until Bordeaux viewing/finish plan is finalized.
logistics.transit: Central Bordeaux; verify tram and pedestrian access under race operations.
logistics.parking: Unknown; central parking likely constrained on Stage 7.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No restaurant-specific closure note found; Bordeaux stage-finish closures may affect access.

timing.best_visit_window: Stage 7 dinner or another Bordeaux night with a reservation.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium-high on Friday July 10 because Bordeaux finish crowds and closures may affect arrival.

sources.title: La Tupina official website
sources.note: Supports address, phone, reservation link, hours, founding date, chef, and Southwest cuisine positioning.

sources.title: Decanter Bordeaux food and drink guide
sources.note: Supports La Tupina as a traditional Bordeaux/Southwest dining recommendation and provides local food context.

open_questions: Confirm reservation availability for Friday July 10, 2026.
Check Bordeaux stage-finish road closures and tram operations before choosing lunch vs dinner.
